text: A striking feature of the task-positive network is its dependence on challenge. Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi observed that optimal engagement — the state he called flow — occurs at the precise boundary where skill and difficulty are balanced. Neuroimaging studies have since shown that this state corresponds to sustained task-positive network dominance with near-complete default mode suppression. Tasks too easy bore the system into DMN reactivation; tasks too hard overwhelm it, triggering anxiety and self-monitoring. The narrow corridor of flow is, neurologically speaking, the zone where the self-model quiets and the task-positive network can hold continuous command without competition.
question: According to the passage, why do tasks that are too easy fail to sustain the flow state?

options.text: They reduce dopamine availability in the striatum
They cause the salience network to misfire
They bore the system into DMN reactivation
They over-activate the anterior insula, disrupting focus
